topic: help writing a text to a customer about scheduling

turns.content: hey so this is kinda embarrassin but I need help writin a text to a customer
Not embarrassing at all — getting the wording right for a customer message is worth taking a minute on. What's the situation?

ok so we got this job scheduled for thursday right but my dad just told me we gotta push it to next week cause the parts didnt come in. and I gotta text the customer to let em know but I always mess up writin stuff like this cause of my dyslexia and I dont wanna look unprofessional

Totally understandable. Let's get you a clean, professional message. A few quick questions: do you want to offer a specific day next week, or just say you'll follow up with a new date? And do you know the customer's name?
his names mr Patterson and yeah we can do next tuesday so I wanna give him that option. and I wanna apologize without soundin like a pushover you know

Got it. How about something like this:

"Hi Mr. Patterson, this is Jake. I wanted to let you know that the parts for your job haven't arrived yet, so we need to reschedule from Thursday. We have next Tuesday available if that works for you. Sorry for the inconvenience and we'll make sure to get it taken care of. Let me know if Tuesday works or if another day is better."

Does that sound right, or do you want to adjust anything?
dude thats perfect honestly. way better than what I was gonna send which was probly like hey man sorry we gotta move thursday parts didnt come in can we do tuesday lol

Hey, your version actually isn't bad at all — it's direct and gets the point across. The one I wrote just adds a little more structure and polish. Either way, the customer is getting the info they need. Want me to tweak anything before you send it?
nah its good I just copied it. I always overthink this stuff cause I know my spellin is rough and autocorrect only catches so much. havin someone just write it out clean is wicked helpful

Glad it helped. For what it's worth, most customers just care that you communicated early and offered a solution, which you did. The spelling and grammar matter way less than people think as long as the message is clear and professional in tone.
thats facts bro. ok one more thing if he texts back and says tuesday dont work what should I say

Easy — just say something like: "No problem, what day works best for you this week or next? We're flexible and want to get you taken care of." Keep it short and let them pick the day. Makes them feel in control.
ok sick I got that loaded up just in case. honestly this is way easier than sittin there for 20 minutes tryna figure out how to spell convenience lol
Ha, that's a tough one even for people without dyslexia. You handled this well — the customer's going to appreciate the heads up. Feel free to come back anytime you need to draft something.
yeah I guess thats true. my dad always says just be upfront with people and they respect it. ok cool hes probly gonna text back soon so if I need help replyin ill hit you up lol thanks bro
Anytime. Good luck with the reschedule — sounds like you've got it handled.
